data.title: Match group announces AI-driven dating features and $100M annual cost savings for 2025
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Match Group (MTCH) Q1 2025

Management View

  • CEO Spencer Rascoff highlighted a major organizational restructure at Match Group, centralizing key operations and reducing the workforce by 13%, with a goal to realize $100 million in annualized cost savings, including $45 million in 2025. He emphasized the shift to a unified, product-led organization to enhance innovation and user outcomes.
  • The company is integrating AI deeper into its platforms, unveiling features like the "Double Date" feature in Europe and "The Game Game," a voice-based AI interaction tool on Tinder. These initiatives aim to drive engagement and reactivations, targeting younger demographics.
  • New AI-enabled discovery tools being tested in New Zealand use personal attributes and dynamic user inputs to generate curated matches, representing a significant leap in personalized dating experiences.
  • CFO Steven Bailey reported Q1 total revenue of $831 million, noting a 3% year-over-year decline. He attributed the decline to falling payers and FX headwinds but highlighted strong indirect revenue growth and cost management as positive offsets.

Outlook

  • Match Group reiterated its full-year 2025 revenue guidance of $3.375 billion to $3.5 billion, unchanged since the last call. CFO Bailey emphasized potential risks from macroeconomic factors and FX volatility.
  • The company plans to accelerate international expansion, with Hinge targeting launches in Brazil and Mexico later this year, and The League entering the Middle East and India.
  • Tinder’s U.S. launch of the "Double Date" feature is scheduled for later this year, with expansions planned for Europe, Asia, and Latin America.
  • Planned restructuring costs of $17 million in Q2 are expected to impact margins temporarily, but the company aims for AOI of $295 million to $300 million in Q2, with a margin of 35%.

Financial Results

  • Tinder reported Q1 direct revenue of $447 million, down 7% year-over-year, with payers declining by 6% to 9.1 million. Monthly active users also fell 9% year-over-year.
  • Hinge continued its growth trajectory, with direct revenue reaching $152 million, a 23% year-over-year increase, driven by a 19% rise in payers and subscription price optimizations.
  • Match Group Asia faced challenges, with direct revenue falling 11% year-over-year to $64 million, affected by FX and market competition in Japan. However, payers increased by 5% year-over-year.
  • Operating income for the quarter was $193 million, representing a 42% margin, while adjusted operating income (AOI) was $275 million, with a margin of 33%.

Q&A

  • Nathan Feather, Morgan Stanley: Asked about balancing cost reductions with investments to maximize productivity. CEO Rascoff explained that savings from restructuring would fund growth in international expansion and product innovation.
  • Cory Carpenter, JPMorgan: Inquired about CEO Rascoff’s priorities. Rascoff outlined a focus on unifying Match Group’s brands, enhancing Tinder’s product roadmap, and fostering a product-oriented culture.
  • Shweta Khajuria, Wolfe Research: Questioned macroeconomic impacts on pricing strategies. Rascoff noted early signs of weakening a la carte revenue at Tinder, particularly among younger users, and mentioned plans for pricing adjustments to mitigate impacts.
  • Ben Black, Deutsche Bank: Asked about Tinder’s AI discovery tool testing in New Zealand. Rascoff highlighted promising early feedback on match quality and brand perception as key outcomes.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed cautious optimism, probing cost management and growth strategies. Questions reflected concerns about Tinder’s declining MAU and payers, as well as the macroeconomic environment.
  • CEO Rascoff projected confidence in addressing challenges, emphasizing bold organizational changes and product innovation. His tone indicated urgency and a focus on long-term growth.
  • Compared to last quarter, the tone showed heightened activity and a clearer strategic direction under Rascoff’s leadership.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • The current quarter showcased a decisive shift in strategy with significant organizational restructuring and cost-cutting measures, unlike the previous quarter's focus on stabilizing operational trends.
  • Tinder’s MAU decline remained consistent at 9%, with new features like "The Game Game" and AI-enabled matching aiming to reverse this trend.
  • Analysts maintained a similar critical tone, focusing on the same key challenges: Tinder’s growth trajectory and macroeconomic concerns.
  • Management’s tone was more aligned with strategic execution, compared to last quarter’s focus on foundational changes.

Risks and Concerns

  • Analysts highlighted risks related to Tinder’s slowing user base, macroeconomic pressures, and FX volatility. Concerns were also raised about the potential impact of new trust and safety features on user growth.
  • Management acknowledged these risks, citing restructuring and product innovation as proactive mitigation strategies. They emphasized the importance of trust and safety measures to improve long-term brand perception and user satisfaction.

Final Takeaway

Match Group’s Q1 2025 earnings call highlighted a transformative quarter under new CEO Spencer Rascoff, focusing on organizational restructuring, cost efficiency, and enhanced product innovation. With $100 million in expected annualized savings, international expansion, and AI-driven features, the company aims to address declining user engagement and revenue trends, particularly at Tinder. Management reiterated confidence in achieving full-year guidance, despite macroeconomic challenges.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: ConocoPhillips outlines $0.5B capital reduction amid guidance for 2025
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: ConocoPhillips (COP) Q1 2025

Management View

  • CEO Ryan Lance highlighted the volatile macroeconomic environment, noting challenges including revised global economic growth projections and softened oil prices. He emphasized ConocoPhillips' resilience, citing its diverse portfolio and inventory below a $40 per barrel WTI cost of supply threshold. He also mentioned a $0.5 billion reduction in capital spending and a $200 million cut in operating costs without impacting production guidance.
  • Lance reported progress in integrating the Marathon Oil acquisition ahead of schedule, contributing to cost reductions and capital efficiency improvements.
  • CFO Bill Bullock stated that production reached 2.389 million barrels of oil equivalent per day in Q1 2025, exceeding guidance. He confirmed adjusted earnings of $2.09 per share and operating cash flow of $5.5 billion for the quarter.
  • Bullock announced his retirement after 39 years, with Andy O’Brien succeeding him as CFO.

Outlook

  • Full-year production guidance remains unchanged, targeting low single-digit growth with reduced capital spending of $12.3 billion to $12.6 billion.
  • Q2 2025 production is expected to range between 2.34 million and 2.38 million barrels of oil equivalent per day, with planned turnarounds impacting 40,000 barrels per day.
  • Operating costs guidance has been reduced to $10.7 billion to $10.9 billion for the year due to cost optimization efforts.
  • Full-year APLNG distributions are revised to $800 million, with $600 million expected in Q3 2025.

Financial Results

  • Q1 2025 production performance reached 2.389 million barrels of oil equivalent per day, including strong contributions from the Permian, Eagle Ford, and Bakken basins.
  • Capital expenditures for the quarter were $3.4 billion. Shareholder returns totaled $2.5 billion, split between $1.5 billion in buybacks and $1 billion in dividends.
  • Cash and short-term investments stood at $7.5 billion at the end of the quarter, with an additional $1 billion in long-term liquid investments.

Q&A

  • Neil Mehta, Goldman Sachs: Asked about the feasibility of the $10 billion capital return target amid a weaker macro environment. CEO Lance responded that the distribution framework is sustainable, and reductions in Q2 capital return reflect current pricing, with flexibility to adjust based on future conditions.
  • Devin McDermott, Morgan Stanley: Inquired about the drivers of the capital reduction. CFO-designate Andy O’Brien attributed it to efficiency improvements, plan optimization, and ongoing synergy realization from the Marathon acquisition.
  • Nitin Kumar, Mizuho: Sought updates on Willow project milestones. Senior VP Kirk Johnson reported progress in infrastructure development, with completion of critical winter construction activities and procurement on track to meet the 2029 first oil target.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed cautious optimism, focusing on the company’s ability to maintain guidance amid macroeconomic pressures. Some analysts raised concerns about potential impacts of a prolonged low-price environment on shareholder returns and capital allocation.
  • Management maintained a confident tone, emphasizing the strength of their portfolio and operational efficiencies. Comments highlighted the company’s resilience and readiness to adjust plans as needed.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Capital spending guidance was reduced from $12.9 billion in Q4 2024 to $12.3 billion to $12.6 billion in Q1 2025, reflecting efficiency gains and plan optimization.
  • Operating costs guidance decreased by $200 million compared to prior forecasts.
  • Production guidance remained consistent, with continued focus on cost reductions and operational improvements.
  • Analysts' questions in Q1 2025 showed a shift towards understanding cost optimizations and macro-level risks, whereas Q4 2024 questions focused more on the integration of Marathon assets.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management acknowledged macroeconomic uncertainty, including volatile oil prices and global economic growth challenges.
  • Analysts highlighted potential risks in achieving capital return targets amid ongoing market volatility.

Final Takeaway

ConocoPhillips delivered strong operational results in Q1 2025, exceeding production guidance while reducing capital and operating costs. Management emphasized flexibility and resilience in navigating a challenging macroeconomic environment, with integration of Marathon Oil assets progressing ahead of schedule. The company remains focused on delivering shareholder returns and sustaining production growth through strategic cost management and efficiency improvements.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Global net lease targets $3B asset sales in 2025 amid deleveraging strategy
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Global Net Lease (GNL) Q1 2025

Management View

  • CEO Michael Weil highlighted the company's progress in its strategic transformation, including the sale of 59 unencumbered properties for $1.1 billion, reducing leverage by paying down $850 million on the revolving credit facility. An additional $700 million in proceeds from the sale of 41 encumbered properties is expected by the end of Q2 2025.

  • The company has repurchased 7.9 million shares at an average price of $7.50, totaling $59 million. A $300 million share repurchase program remains in effect.

  • CFO Chris Masterson reported first-quarter revenue of $132.4 million and an AFFO of $66.2 million, or $0.29 per share. The company reduced its gross outstanding debt balance to $3.9 billion and achieved a net debt to adjusted EBITDA ratio of 6.7 times.

Outlook

  • The company reaffirmed its 2025 AFFO per share guidance range of $0.90 to $0.96 and expects a net debt to adjusted EBITDA ratio between 6.5 times and 7.1 times.

  • Plans include completing $3 billion in asset sales by the end of 2025, streamlining the portfolio to approximately $5.5 billion in value.

  • The multi-tenant portfolio sale is expected to enhance credit ratings, with Fitch and S&P placing GNL on credit watch positive.

Financial Results

  • Revenue for Q1 2025 was $132.4 million, with a net loss attributable to common stockholders of $200.3 million. AFFO reached $66.2 million, or $0.29 per share.

  • Debt reduction efforts have lowered the gross outstanding debt to $3.9 billion, with 91% of debt fixed at a weighted average interest rate of 4.4%.

  • Liquidity as of March 31, 2025, stood at $499 million, with $1.4 billion available on the credit facility.

Q&A

  • John Kim, BMO Capital Markets, questioned the impact of market volatility on asset sales and pricing. CEO Weil indicated stable market conditions and effective broker relationships.

  • Upal Rana, KeyBanc Capital Markets, asked about the priorities for capital allocation. CEO Weil emphasized leverage reduction and opportunistic share buybacks.

  • Mitch Germain, Citizens JMP, inquired about the strategic focus post-dispositions. CEO Weil stated the company’s focus on streamlining the portfolio and maintaining strong liquidity.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed cautious optimism, focusing on the execution of asset sales and financial discipline. Concerns were raised about market volatility and future strategic moves.

  • Management appeared confident, emphasizing progress in deleveraging and strategic transformation. CEO Weil repeatedly stressed disciplined execution and long-term value creation.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • AFFO per share decreased from $0.34 in Q4 2024 to $0.29 in Q1 2025, reflecting the impacts of asset sales and strategic shifts.

  • The debt reduction progress accelerated, with net debt to adjusted EBITDA improving from 7.6 times at the end of 2024 to 6.7 times at the end of Q1 2025.

  • Strategic focus shifted to completing the multi-tenant portfolio sale and reducing exposure to non-core sectors like gas and convenience stores.

Risks and Concerns

  • Market volatility due to tariffs and economic uncertainty may impact asset sales and pricing.

  • Exposure to struggling sectors like gas and convenience stores is being mitigated through strategic scaling back.

  • The departure of Contractor’s Steel impacted occupancy but was addressed through asset sales, maintaining portfolio stability.

Final Takeaway

Global Net Lease continues to execute its strategic transformation, focusing on deleveraging and streamlining its portfolio. The company aims to complete $3 billion in asset sales by the end of 2025, further strengthening its financial position and improving credit metrics. Management's disciplined approach, including targeted share repurchases and a focus on high-quality, investment-grade tenants, positions GNL for long-term growth and resilience.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Pan American Silver highlights $112.6M free cash flow in Q1 2025 with strong production and cost performance
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Pan American Silver Corp. (PAAS) Q1 2025

Management View

  • CEO Michael Steinmann noted a strong start to 2025 with record mine operating earnings of $250.8 million, driven by improved metal prices and cost management. He emphasized La Colorada's performance, where enhanced ventilation improved development rates, throughput, and reduced costs.
  • Steinmann highlighted that silver production of over 5 million ounces slightly exceeded guidance, while all-in sustaining costs (AISC) for the silver segment were $13.94 per ounce, below the guided range. This was attributed to lower production costs and higher byproduct credits from increased gold, zinc, and lead production.
  • Gold production reached 182,200 ounces, meeting guidance, with AISC at $1,485 per ounce, also better than expected due to higher silver byproduct credits and residual leaching at Dolores.
  • CFO Ignacio Couturier reported Q1 revenue of $773 million, net earnings of $169 million (or $0.47 per share), and adjusted earnings of $153 million (or $0.42 per share). Operating cash flow before noncash working capital changes was $240 million, and free cash flow reached $112.6 million.
  • Steinmann announced advancements in the La Colorada Skarn project, with ongoing engineering, exploration, and partnership discussions. The company aims to retain maximum silver exposure from this deposit.

Outlook

  • Management reaffirmed its annual guidance, maintaining the February-provided consolidated production, cost, and expenditure forecasts. Production is expected to increase in the second half of 2025.
  • CEO Steinmann highlighted that favorable metal prices and exchange rates could positively influence costs, with continued strong cost control efforts.
  • Discussions for the Escobal operation in Guatemala are progressing, with additional meetings anticipated in the coming months, but no completion date for the consultation process has been set.

Financial Results

  • Q1 revenue was $773 million, with net earnings of $169 million (or $0.47 per share) and adjusted earnings of $153 million (or $0.42 per share).
  • The company achieved free cash flow of $112.6 million, driven by strong operational performance and cost efficiencies.
  • Cash and short-term investments increased to $923 million, contributing to total liquidity of $1.7 billion when including the undrawn credit line.
  • Capital allocation included $81 million in sustaining and growth projects and $56 million returned to shareholders through dividends and share buybacks.

Q&A

  • Ovais Habib, Scotiabank: Questioned if improved cost performance in Q1 would adjust subsequent guidance. CEO Steinmann and SVP Scott Campbell indicated no immediate adjustments but expressed confidence in sustaining current performance levels.
  • Cosmos Chiu, CIBC: Asked about challenges at Bell Creek and Minera Florida. COO Steve Busby and SVP Campbell explained ongoing seismicity management at Bell Creek and operational impacts of absenteeism in Chile, with improvements expected in the latter half of the year.
  • Don DeMarco, National Bank: Inquired about capital allocation priorities. CEO Steinmann emphasized shareholder returns through dividends and buybacks and investments in exploration and growth projects like the La Colorada Skarn.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts highlighted strong cost control and operational performance, with a positive tone in their questions about future cost stability and production guidance.
  • Management displayed confidence during prepared remarks, emphasizing strong liquidity and operational improvements. However, cautious optimism was evident in discussions about uncertainties like Escobal.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, both analysts and management maintained a similar tone, focusing on operational stability and strategic growth.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Q1 2025 saw record mine operating earnings of $250.8 million, up from Q4 2024's $196.2 million free cash flow reflecting operational improvements and increased metal prices.
  • Guidance remained unchanged, with production expected to be second-half weighted, consistent with prior patterns.
  • Management maintained a positive tone on operational and financial performance, with a continued focus on cost control and shareholder returns.

Risks and Concerns

  • Challenges at Bell Creek related to seismicity and ground conditions were highlighted but are being managed with dynamic support systems.
  • Absenteeism and equipment delays impacted Minera Florida, with recovery expected later in the year.
  • The consultation process for Escobal remains uncertain, with no timeline for completion or restart of operations.

Final Takeaway

Pan American Silver Corp. delivered strong financial and operational results in Q1 2025, with record mine operating earnings and robust free cash flow underpinned by cost efficiencies and improved production. The company reaffirmed its annual guidance and highlighted strategic investments in growth projects like the La Colorada Skarn. Management remains optimistic about sustaining performance, supported by favorable metal prices and exchange rates, while addressing operational challenges to ensure continued shareholder value creation.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: ACM Research anticipates 15% revenue growth in 2025 amid global semiconductor expansion
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: ACM Research (ACMR) Q1 2025

Management View

  • CEO David Wang highlighted ACM Research's inclusion among the Top 20 Global Semiconductor Equipment Companies for 2024, emphasizing the company's strategic positioning in the semiconductor industry. He detailed the company's market share in China, with over 25% in wafer cleaning and cleaning products, translating to more than 9% of the global market.
  • Wang outlined the company's advancements, including the recognition of its panel-level packaging tool, which received the 3D InCites Technology Enablement Award. This tool is designed for AI chip packaging, a growing area of demand.
  • ACM is expanding its global footprint, notably with investments in a 40,000 square foot facility in Oregon, aimed at supporting U.S.-based production and reducing tariff uncertainties.
  • CFO Mark McKechnie reported revenue growth of 13% year-over-year, totaling $172.3 million, and highlighted a gross margin of 48.2%, which exceeded the company's target range.

Outlook

  • Management reaffirmed their 2025 revenue outlook in the range of $850 million to $950 million, implying 15% year-over-year growth at the midpoint.
  • CEO Wang noted anticipated growth in shipment volumes for the rest of 2025, despite a challenging comparison to the strong shipments in the prior year.
  • Plans to enhance product offerings, including high-temperature cleaning tools and advanced packaging solutions, were mentioned as key drivers for future growth.

Financial Results

  • Revenue for Q1 2025 was reported at $172.3 million, a 13.2% increase from the same period in 2024.
  • Gross margin reached 48.2%, above the long-term target range of 42% to 48%.
  • Net income attributable to ACM Research was $31.3 million, with diluted earnings per share of $0.46.
  • Cash flow from operations turned positive at $5.3 million, compared to a negative $9 million in Q1 2024.
  • Capital expenditures for the quarter were $17.1 million, with an annual expectation of $70 million.

Q&A

  • Charles Shi, Needham & Company, asked about shipment growth expectations for the full year. CEO Wang and CFO McKechnie confirmed expectations for shipment growth but clarified it may not match the revenue growth rate.
  • Shi inquired about the impact of tariffs and export controls. Management explained that sourcing from third countries and local suppliers has minimized these impacts.
  • Questions about competition in China's semiconductor market were addressed, with Wang emphasizing ACM's technological innovation and IP protection as key competitive strengths.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed some skepticism regarding shipment projections and the impact of geopolitical factors, reflecting a slightly negative sentiment in their questioning.
  • Management maintained a confident tone, particularly regarding their competitive positioning and product innovation, signaling a positive sentiment during both prepared remarks and Q&A exchanges.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management's tone showed consistent confidence in navigating market challenges and executing growth strategies.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Revenue grew 13.2% year-over-year but declined from the previous quarter's $223.5 million, largely due to high shipment volumes in Q4 2024.
  • Gross margin improved over the target range, indicating effective cost management and favorable product mix.
  • The reaffirmation of the 2025 revenue outlook reflects consistent strategic priorities, with no significant changes in guidance language or focus areas.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management acknowledged the challenges posed by geopolitical uncertainties, including tariffs and export controls. Mitigation strategies include diversifying supply chains and localizing production.
  • Concerns regarding competition in the Chinese market were addressed with confidence in ACM's unique technological capabilities and strong IP protection.

Final Takeaway

ACM Research demonstrated strong financial performance in Q1 2025, exceeding gross margin targets and maintaining its revenue growth trajectory. The company is actively expanding its global footprint and investing in innovative technologies to address evolving semiconductor industry demands. Management's confidence in achieving 2025 revenue targets is supported by a robust product pipeline and strategic investments in production capabilities. Analysts raised concerns about shipment growth and geopolitical risks, but the company’s mitigation strategies and market positioning suggest resilience.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: AB InBev targets 4% to 8% EBITDA growth for 2025 amid brand momentum
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Anheuser-Busch InBev (BUD) Q1 2025

Management View

  • CEO Michel Doukeris highlighted a 7.9% EBITDA growth in Q1 2025, reaching the top end of the company's outlook range. The company saw margin expansion, with a 7% increase in underlying U.S. dollar EPS and 20% growth in constant currency terms.
  • The U.S. portfolio showed momentum, with Michelob Ultra and Busch Light achieving the top two volume share gains in the beer industry for the second consecutive quarter.
  • Revenue from the no-alcohol beer portfolio increased by 34%, driven by strong growth of Corona Cero.
  • BEES platform gross merchandise value grew by 53% year-over-year to $645 million.
  • CFO Fernando Tennenbaum reported underlying EPS of $0.81, a 7.1% increase in U.S. dollars, driven by organic EBITDA growth and cost efficiencies.

Outlook

  • CEO Doukeris reiterated confidence in the company's 2025 outlook of 4% to 8% EBITDA growth, citing the resilience of the beer category and the strength of its mega brands.
  • Management emphasized plans to activate the category further through global platforms like the NBA, FIFA Club World Cup, and Corona's 100th anniversary celebrations.
  • The company expects industry improvement in the U.S. during the summer, supported by brand activations and an extensive innovation pipeline.

Financial Results

  • Revenue increased by 1.5%, supported by a 3.7% rise in revenue per hectoliter despite a 2.2% volume decline due to calendar-related factors.
  • EBITDA margin improved by 218 basis points with expansion in four of five operating regions.
  • BEES platform generated $11.6 billion in GMV, a 10% increase year-over-year, showcasing the expansion of digital capabilities.
  • Digital direct-to-consumer platforms contributed $117 million in revenue, a 12% increase year-over-year.

Q&A

  • Robert Ottenstein, Evercore ISI: Asked about sustainability of U.S. market share gains and investment rationale in a mature market. CEO Doukeris emphasized the long-term growth potential and the company's multiyear investment program to support portfolio growth.
  • Sanjeet Aujla, UBS: Queried about China’s channel mix and group-level volume growth potential. Doukeris noted sequential industry improvement in China and highlighted opportunities in the off-trade channel, alongside increased investments in mega brands.
  • Simon Hales, Citi: Inquired about consumer behavior dynamics and U.S. innovations. Doukeris mentioned the resilience of the beer category and highlighted Michelob Ultra Zero as the #1 innovation in the U.S. beer category this year.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts: Neutral to slightly positive tone, with a focus on U.S. market dynamics and China’s recovery. Questions reflected interest in growth sustainability and margin performance.
  • Management: Positive and confident, particularly on U.S. portfolio momentum and execution in strategic markets. Repeated phrases like "we are confident" and "we are encouraged" signaled optimism.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management maintained a consistent tone, emphasizing long-term growth and operational efficiency.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Q1 2025 showed continued margin expansion and a strong digital platform performance, building on the momentum from Q4 2024.
  • Strategic focus shifted slightly, with increased emphasis on digital platforms and balanced choice portfolios.
  • Analysts continued to probe about China, indicating ongoing concerns about market recovery and execution in the region.

Risks and Concerns

  • Calendar effects and adverse weather impacted U.S. and Mexico volumes.
  • Challenges in China persist, with regional and channel-specific underperformance.
  • Macro pressures in certain markets, including currency devaluations, could weigh on margins in the second half of the year.

Final Takeaway

AB InBev demonstrated strong execution in Q1 2025, with EBITDA growth at the top of the outlook range, solid performance in digital platforms, and momentum in key markets like the U.S. The company remains confident in achieving its 2025 EBITDA growth target of 4% to 8%, supported by its portfolio of mega brands and strategic investments in innovation and market activation.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: American cardinal selected as new pope of Catholic Church
data.description:
White smoke emerged from the chimney of the Sistine Chapel just after 6 p.m. Thursday evening in Rome -- the traditional signal that Roman Catholic cardinals selected a new pope to lead the 1.3 billion-member global church.

Robert Francis Prevost won election as the Catholic Church's 267th pope on the second day of a papal conclave that called for winning two-thirds support among 133 voting cardinals.

The 69-year-old Chicago-born cardinal chose the papal name Leo XIV.

It was considered a bit of an unlikely outcome considering the Trump administration's moves toward cutting worldwide aid and its approach to large-scale deportations, stances at odds with those of the Church.

Prevost became the Vatican's top official for selecting bishops around the globe.

While the election of popes isn't directly linked to financial markets, it can have an effect on euro currency markets, given the economic significance of the Vatican and Italy.

Meanwhile, the Catholic Church has an outsize effect on international relations (and particularly developed vs. emerging markets) depending on where it focuses its significant diplomatic efforts. The Church also has an impact on environmental, social and governance questions amid potential critiques of capitalism.
